# Card

Groups related content and actions on a distinct surface.

## Contents

- [Installation](#installation)
- [Usage](#usage)
- [Header actions](#header-actions)
- [Content patterns](#content-patterns)
- [Semantics and layout](#semantics-and-layout)
- [When to use](#when-to-use)

## Installation

Add Card from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/card
```

## Usage

Compose only the regions the content needs. `CardHeader` arranges the title, description, and optional action; `CardContent` owns the main body; and `CardFooter` is for concluding actions or metadata.

```vue
<template>
  <Card class="w-full max-w-sm">
    <CardHeader>
      <CardTitle>Create project</CardTitle>
      <CardDescription>
        Deploy your new project in one click.
      </CardDescription>
    </CardHeader>
    <CardContent>
      <form class="grid gap-4">
        <div class="grid gap-1.5">
          <Label for="project-name">Name</Label>
          <Input
            id="project-name"
            placeholder="Name of your project"
          />
        </div>
        <div class="grid gap-1.5">
          <Label for="framework">Framework</Label>
          <Select>
            <SelectTrigger
              id="framework"
              class="w-full"
            >
              <SelectValue placeholder="Select a framework" />
            </SelectTrigger>
            <SelectContent>
              <SelectItem value="nuxt">Nuxt</SelectItem>
              <SelectItem value="next">Next.js</SelectItem>
              <SelectItem value="sveltekit">SvelteKit</SelectItem>
              <SelectItem value="astro">Astro</SelectItem>
            </SelectContent>
          </Select>
        </div>
      </form>
    </CardContent>
    <CardFooter class="justify-between">
      <Button variant="outline">
        Cancel
      </Button>
      <Button>Deploy</Button>
    </CardFooter>
  </Card>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Button } from '@/Components/Button'
import {
  Card,
  CardContent,
  CardDescription,
  CardFooter,
  CardHeader,
  CardTitle,
} from '@/Components/Card'
import { Input } from '@/Components/Input'
import { Label } from '@/Components/Label'
import {
  Select,
  SelectContent,
  SelectItem,
  SelectTrigger,
  SelectValue,
} from '@/Components/Select'
</script>
```

## Header actions

Place a compact, secondary action in `CardAction`. It occupies a dedicated header column, so keep its label short and avoid putting a full action toolbar there. On narrow cards, verify that the title and action can coexist without squeezing important text.

**Header action example**

```vue
<template>
  <Card class="w-full max-w-sm">
    <CardHeader>
      <CardTitle>Workspace</CardTitle>
      <CardDescription>Manage members and workspace settings.</CardDescription>
      <CardAction>
        <Button variant="outline" size="sm">Settings</Button>
      </CardAction>
    </CardHeader>
    <CardContent class="text-sm text-muted-foreground">
      12 members · Pro plan
    </CardContent>
  </Card>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Button } from '@/Components/Button'
import {
  Card,
  CardAction,
  CardContent,
  CardDescription,
  CardHeader,
  CardTitle,
} from '@/Components/Card'
</script>
```

## Content patterns

Cards may contain forms, summaries, or compact collections, but the card itself does not own their state. Keep validation with form controls and keep fetched data, loading, and mutation state in the parent feature.

**Notifications example**

```vue
<template>
  <Card class="w-full max-w-sm">
    <CardHeader>
      <CardTitle>Notifications</CardTitle>
      <CardDescription>You have 3 unread messages.</CardDescription>
    </CardHeader>
    <CardContent class="grid gap-4">
      <div class="flex items-center gap-4 rounded-md border p-4">
        <BellIcon class="size-5" />
        <div class="flex-1 space-y-1">
          <p class="text-sm font-medium leading-none">
            Push notifications
          </p>
          <p class="text-sm text-muted-foreground">
            Send notifications to this device.
          </p>
        </div>
        <Switch />
      </div>
      <div>
        <div
          v-for="notification in notifications"
          :key="notification.title"
          class="mb-4 grid grid-cols-[25px_minmax(0,1fr)] items-start pb-4 last:mb-0 last:pb-0"
        >
          <span class="flex size-2 translate-y-1 rounded-full bg-sky-500" />
          <div class="space-y-1">
            <p class="text-sm font-medium leading-none">
              {{ notification.title }}
            </p>
            <p class="text-sm text-muted-foreground">
              {{ notification.description }}
            </p>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </CardContent>
    <CardFooter>
      <Button class="w-full">
        <CheckIcon />
        Mark all as read
      </Button>
    </CardFooter>
  </Card>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Button } from '@/Components/Button'
import {
  Card,
  CardContent,
  CardDescription,
  CardFooter,
  CardHeader,
  CardTitle,
} from '@/Components/Card'
import { Switch } from '@/Components/Switch'
import { BellIcon, CheckIcon } from '@lucide/vue'

const notifications = [
  {
    title: 'Your call has been confirmed.',
    description: '1 hour ago',
  },
  {
    title: 'You have a new message!',
    description: '1 hour ago',
  },
  {
    title: 'Your subscription is expiring soon.',
    description: '2 hours ago',
  },
]
</script>
```

## Semantics and layout

`CardTitle` renders an `h3`. Ensure that level fits the surrounding page outline; if it does not, use an appropriate heading outside the card and treat the card title as supporting text. Card regions are visual `div` containers, so add a semantic element around the card when it represents an article, section, or form.

Let the parent grid decide card width. Equal-height cards are useful in comparison grids, but do not force equal heights when content length is unpredictable. Footer borders and header borders receive matching spacing when a `border-t` or `border-b` class is added.

## When to use

- Use Card for a self-contained summary, setting group, or focused workflow.
- Avoid wrapping every page section in a card; spacing and headings are often enough.
- Use Item for repeated compact rows and Panel for read-only name-value details.

Hosted documentation: [Card](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/card)
